page: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-32 is a memory-optimized server instance powered by dedicated Intel x86_64 processors. It features 32 virtual CPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system is equipped with 256 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core. The CPU architecture includes a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It supports advanced instruction sets including Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512). This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and has zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates strong memory subsystem performance, with uncached large-block memory read bandwidth reaching 208.51 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 99.59 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25% of tested servers. In compute benchmarks, the instance delivers average single-core and multi-core performance, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 15821.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 38513.51. It demonstrates top-tier performance in text generation for small LLM models, achieving 328.98 tokens/sec, alongside strong static web serving throughput of 44.59 GB/sec.

Given its high memory density and dedicated Intel compute resources, the n4-highmem-32 offers a balanced cost-to-resource profile for memory-intensive workloads without the overhead of local storage or GPU hardware. The presence of AMX and AVX-512 instructions makes it highly capable for CPU-based matrix operations and vector calculations. This server is well-suited for memory-bound applications, database operations, static web serving, software compilation, and small-scale LLM inference tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-32 is a memory-optimized server featuring 32 vCPUs and 256 GB of RAM, yielding an 8 GB per core memory ratio. Powered by an Intel x86_64 processor with a 260 MB L3 cache, it supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel AMX. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs. In performance testing, it demonstrates strong uncached memory bandwidth, with read speeds of 208.51 GB/sec and write speeds of 99.59 GB/sec, alongside top-tier text generation speeds for small LLM models. It also delivers strong static web serving throughput of 44.59 GB/sec. This configuration is optimized for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, and CPU-based inference tasks.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-32 is a memory-optimized server with 32 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and dedicated Intel processors. It delivers strong memory bandwidth and is optimized for database and memory-bound workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-32 features 32 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and Intel AMX support, optimized for memory-intensive workloads and databases.
tagline: A memory-optimized server featuring dedicated Intel processors, high uncached memory bandwidth, and advanced matrix extension support for memory-intensive workloads.

Dedicated Intel x86_64 processor with AVX-512 and AMX support
